title: Don't skip rootless storage setup for ps if --size set
body:
We need the store to look up sizes for containers.

Fixes #2824

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@giuseppe PTAL
Let's not merge this without his OK - I don't know why we set SkipStorageSetup here in the first place
@edsantiago as the reporter, care to comment if this makes your tests happy?
`Error: error creating libpod runtime: chown /home/esm/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l: operation not permitted`
New code fixes the reported behavior and passes my BATS test. I don't have a setup for running docker-autotest on built binaries, so it'll take me a while to get that going. So far, LGTM.

bot, retest this please
We need @giuseppe review of this one.
Passes docker-autotest as nonroot. LGTM. Thanks for the quick fix.
Lots of ps related failures in rootless testing. Not sure what's going on here. Seems to pass locally?
Would it make sense to add a tiny test for this, since this seems trivial to reproduce: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2824#issue-427829035?
I'm actually really curious as to why our existing test suite didn't catch this one - it seems like the rootless tests ought to be doing a `ps --size` already
@giuseppe Can you take a look at this? The failures on rootless testing are stumping me

this was one of the issues that convinced me in the refactoring and that should be fixed there.

We'd need to join different namespaces for each container and we cannot do that (unless we re-exec for each container and return back the result).
Aw, hell. OK, I'll close this, then. We'll go with your single userns PR instead.
Is there a simple guide on how to write and run tests?
@baude has a guide on running the tests at https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/README.md
